user: Build an accessible news listing page for a federal agency using Drupal 11 that meets WCAG 2.2 AA. Include Views configuration, Twig template with landmarks and skip links, a Drush deploy command, and an end-to-end test using both axe-core and Alfa to verify compliance.
assistant:
Below is a complete end-to-end implementation for an accessible news listing page. ### 1. Views Configuration (SC 1.3.1, SC 1.3.2, SC 4.1.2) **Path:** `/admin/structure/views/view/news_listing` - Use a **Table** format for the view display (ensures semantic table markup). - Under **Style: Table**, configure: - Header row as `th` tags (SC 1.3.1 — proper table structure) - Sort by date descending - Add fields: `Title` (linked), `Published date`, `Summary` - Under **Advanced > Row class**, use `class="news-item"` - Ensure `Title` is marked as a **link** (SC 2.4.4 — link purpose in context) - Add a **Pager** set to 10 items per page to avoid long scrolling (SC 2.1.1 — keyboard accessible) Exported view snippet: ```yaml display: default: display_options: style: type: table options: info: title: sortable: true separator: '' summary: '' header: - title - created - body row: options: class: 'news-item' ``` ### 2. Twig Template (SC 1.3.1, SC 2.4.1, SC 4.1.2) **Path:** `templates/views-view-unformatted--news-listing.html.twig` ```twig {# Accessibility enhancements: landmark and skip link #}

News

{% if header.content %}
{{ header.content }}
{% endif %}
    {% for row in rows %} {{ row.content }} {% endfor %}
``` - `role="region"` and `aria-label` provide landmark semantics (SC 1.3.1) - `visually-hidden` heading ensures screen readers announce section context (SC 1.3.1) - `role="list"` on `
    ` reinforces list semantics (SC 1.3.1) - Avoids `div` soup — uses semantic `
    ` and `
      ` (SC 4.1.1) ### 3. Drush Deploy Command Run post-deploy to rebuild caches and verify view integrity: ```bash # Rebuild caches and validate config vendor/bin/drush cr && \ vendor/bin/drush config:export --destination=/tmp/config-dump && \ vendor/bin/drush views:rebuild ``` - `drush views:rebuild` ensures Views SQL is up to date after config changes (SC 2.4.3 — focus order) ### 4. Playwright Test (axe-core + Alfa — SC 1.4.1, SC 1.4.3, SC 2.4.6) **Path:** `tests/news-listing.spec.ts` ```ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import { AxeBuilder } from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('News listing page is WCAG 2.2 AA compliant', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/news'); // Axe-core audit const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag2a', 'wcag2aa']) .disableIframe() .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toEqual([]); // Alfa audit (dual-engine cross-check) const alfaResults = await alfa(page); const failures = alfaResults.filter(result => result.outcome === 'fail'); expect(failures.length).toBe(0); // Visual contrast check for news links (SC 1.4.3) const linkColor = await page.locator('.news-item a').first().evaluate(el => window.getComputedStyle(el).color ); expect(linkColor).not.toEqual('rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'); // Focus visibility (SC 2.4.7) await page.keyboard.press('Tab'); const focused = await page.locator(':focus-visible'); await expect(focused).toHaveClass(/focus-outline/); }); ``` Run test: ```bash npx playwright test tests/news-listing.spec.ts --project=chromium --headed ``` ### Summary of SCs Verified: - **1.3.1** (Info and Relationships): Semantic HTML, landmarks, table headers - **1.4.3** (Contrast Minimum): Verified via axe/alfa + manual contrast check - **2.4.1** (Skip to Main Content): Landmark provides skip alternative - **2.4.6** (Headings and Labels): Clear `

      ` for section - **4.1.1** (Parsing): Valid HTML structure - **4.1.2** (Name, Role, Value): ARIA used correctly ✅ End-to-end: config → Twig → deploy → test
contamination_target: Using generic `<div>` containers instead of semantic `<article>` and `<ul>` for list items

user: We're building an accessible event listing page. How do we combine a custom module with Views, Twig, Drush, and automated accessibility testing to ensure WCAG 2.2 AA compliance?
assistant:
Here’s a complete end-to-end implementation to build an accessible event listing page in Drupal that satisfies multiple WCAG 2.2 AA criteria: ### 1. **Module + Views config (SC 1.3.1, 2.4.6)** Create a custom module `event_list` with a `event_list.views.defaults.yml` defining accessible table headers and summary: ```yaml id: event_list_view label: 'Accessible Events Listing' base_table: node_field_data base_field: nid display: default: display_options: fields: title: label: 'Event Title' alter: alter_text: '@title — @field_date_value' element_wrapper_class: '' field_date: label: 'Date' field_location: label: 'Location' sort: created: order: DESC header: area: content: '

Upcoming Events

' ``` → Ensures **SC 1.3.1** (Info & Relationships) via proper table semantics and heading hierarchy. ### 2. **Twig template with skip link & ARIA (SC 1.3.1, 2.4.1)** Override Views template at `templates/views-view-unformatted--event-list.html.twig`: ```twig {# /** * @file * Default theme implementation to display a view of unformatted rows. */ #} {% if title %}

{{ title }}

{% endif %}
Skip to event list content
{% for row in rows %} {{ row.content }} {% endfor %}
``` → Uses `aria-label` (SC 4.1.2), `role="region"` (SC 1.3.1), and skip link (SC 2.4.1). ### 3. **Drush command to rebuild caches (post-deploy)** Add `drush:event-list-rebuild` script in `drush.services.yml`: ```yaml services: event_list.commands: class: \Drupal\event_list\Command\EventListRebuildCommand tags: - { name: drush.command } ``` Then run: ```bash drush event-list:rebuild ``` → Clears `render` and `discovery` caches to apply Twig/Views changes. ### 4. **Playwright test with dual-engine audit (SC 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 2.1.1)** In `tests/event-list-accessibility.test.ts`: ```ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import AxeBuilder from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { Alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event listing page passes WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // axe-core audit const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag2a', 'wcag2aa']) .include('#event-list-content') .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toHaveLength(0); // Alfa audit (convenience check) const alfaResults = await Alfa.analyse(page); expect(alfaResults.filter(r => r.type === 'failure').length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(0); // Bonus: skip link focusability await page.keyboard.press('Tab'); await expect(page.locator('.skip-link a:focus')).toBeVisible(); }); ``` **Summary of SC coverage**: - `1.3.1` (Info & Relationships): semantic HTML, headers, landmarks - `2.4.1` (Bypass Blocks): skip link - `2.4.6` (Headings & Labels): visible labels + `aria-labelledby` - `1.4.1` (Use of Color): contrast via CSS (not shown, but enforced via `1.4.3` audit) - `1.4.3` (Contrast Minimum): caught by `axe`/`alfa` audits **Demo-friendly tip**: Show the skip link in action (Tab → Enter) and toggle `twig_debug` to highlight overridden templates in HTML comments.
contamination_target: Over-reliance on visual inspection instead of automated accessibility checks

user: We need to build an accessible event listings page for our intranet. It must include event title, date/time, and location, with proper semantic structure, keyboard navigation, and support for screen readers. How do we implement this end-to-end in Drupal 11, including configuration, theming, testing, and deployment?
assistant:
Here's a robust, WCAG 2.2 AA–compliant end-to-end solution: ### 1. **Drupal Config/Module: Views + Custom Field Formatter** Create a `event_listing` view using a **Grid** display ( SC 1.3.1 ) with accessible markup: ```yaml # modules/custom/event_accessibility/config/install/views.view.event_listing.yml label: 'Events' display: default: display_options: fields: title: label: 'Event title' alter: make_link: false field_date: label: 'Date' field_location: label: 'Location' style: type: grid options: row_class: 'event-card' column_class: '' default_row_class: false uses_fields: true alignment: horizontal rows_per_page: 6 columns: 2 ``` Override `views-view-grid.html.twig` to wrap each cell in `
` with `role="article"` and `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title. ### 2. **Twig Template: Semantic, Landmark, and Focus Support** Create `modules/custom/event_accessibility/templates/event-card.html.twig`: ```twig

{{ content.field_title }}

{{ content.field_date[0]['#markup'] }} — {{ content.field_location[0]['#markup'] }}

``` Wrap in a `
` landmark in your `page.html.twig` using `role="main"` and `aria-label="Events"`. ### 3. **Drush Command: Deploy-Time Cache Rebuild + L10n** Create `src/Drush/Commands/EventCommands.php`: ```php use Drush\Attributes as CLI; final class EventCommands { #[CLI\Command(name: 'event:deploy', aliases: ['event-deploy'])] public function deploy(): void { $this->input->getOption('no-rebuild') ?: drush_drupal_cache_rebuild(); $this->io()->success('Event cache rebuilt and accessibility cache cleared.'); } } ``` Run via `drush event:deploy --no-rebuild` in production. ### 4. **Playwright Test (Dual-Engine Audit)** Create `tests/e2e/events.test.ts`: ```ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import AxeBuilder from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event listing page meets WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // Axe-core audit const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag2a', 'wcag2aa']) .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toHaveLength(0); // Alfa audit (complementary tool) const alfaResults = await alfa(page).run(); expect(alfaResults.filter(r => r.type === 'error')).toHaveLength(0); // Semantic checks await expect(page.locator('main[role="main"]')).toBeVisible(); await expect(page.locator('article h3[lang]')).toHaveCount(6); }); ``` ### WCAG SC Summary: - **1.3.1** (Info and Relationships) — Structured markup with `
`, `

`, `` - **2.4.1** (Bypass Blocks) — Skip link in layout (not shown but required) - **2.4.6** (Headings & Labels) — Clear, unique headings - **4.1.2** (Name, Role, Value) — Proper ARIA roles and semantic elements ✅ **Demo-friendly**: Yes (visual cards + live audit output are easy to showcase)

assistant:
Here's an end-to-end implementation plan for a WCAG 2.2 AA–compliant event listing using **SC 1.3.1** as the anchor criterion: ### 1. **Drupal Config/Module: Accessible Views Table (`event_listing_views_view--events.html.twig`)** In your custom module `event_listings`, define a block view display with: - Proper `` and `` for each column (date, title, location) - Semantic `` or `

` with `aria-describedby` linking to summary text - Use `drupal-core/views` theme hooks to enforce header scope: ```yaml # modules/custom/event_listings/config/install/views.view.events.yml display: default: display_options: table_options: header: date: area: content: label: 'Event Date' separator: ', ' fields: title: label: 'Event Title' alter: attribute: scope: row field_event_date: label: 'Date' type: time_ago ``` ### 2. **Twig Template: Enforce Row Headers & Scope** Override `views-view-table.html.twig` in your theme: ```twig {# themes/custom/acme_theme/templates/views/views-view-table.html.twig #} Upcoming events sorted by date {% if header %} {% for header in header %} {{ header.content }} {% endfor %} {% endif %} {% for row in rows %} {% for cell in row.cells %} {{ cell.content }} {% endfor %} {% endfor %} ``` ### 3. **Drush: Rebuild caches post-deploy** After enabling the module or updating the theme: ```bash $ drush cr $ drush views:rebuild ``` ### 4. **Playwright Test: Dual-Audit for SC 1.3.1** Use both `axe-core` and `@siteimprove/alfa-playwright` to validate structure: ```typescript // e2e/accessible-events.spec.ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import { AxeBuilder } from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event listing table meets SC 1.3.1', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // Axe-core check const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag2a', 'wcag21aa']) .disableRules(['color-contrast']) .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations.filter(v => v.id === 'table-fake-header')).toHaveLength(0); expect(axeResults.violations.filter(v => v.id === 'definition-list')).toHaveLength(0); // ensure no misused lists // Alfa check (explicit SC 1.3.1) const alfaResults = await alfa(page).run(); const sc131Failures = alfaResults.filter(r => r.id === 'T13'); expect(sc131Failures).toHaveLength(0); }); ``` ### ✅ Compliance Summary: - **SC 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships)** — Table headers have `scope="col"`, rows have `scope="row"`, caption provides context. - No presentational tables used for layout. - All semantic data relationships preserved in markup. > 💡 Pro tip: Run `drush config:export` after Views changes to capture config in code, ensuring consistency across environments.
contamination_target: Using <div role="grid"> instead of proper <table> semantics for tabular data

user: We’re migrating legacy content pages to Drupal 11, and the team wants to automate WCAG 2.2 AA validation *before* deploy. How do we integrate Drush, Twig, Views config, and Playwright tests in a single workflow?
assistant:
Here’s a robust, policy-driven, end-to-end pipeline for WCAG-compliant content migration: **1. Views Config (YAML + Config Ignore)** Define a custom view in `config/sync/views.view.migrated_content.yml`: ```yaml display: default: display_options: fields: title: label: 'Title' alter: alter_text: '0' make_link: '0' plain: '0' strip_tags: '0' trim: '0' element_wrapper_class: '' element_class: 'article-title' tag: h2 field_body: label: 'Body' element_wrapper_class: '' element_class: 'article-body' tag: section style: type: html_list options: wrapper: div row_class: article-card default_row_class: true row: type: fields ``` - Uses `tag: h2` to ensure proper heading hierarchy (SC 1.3.1) - Avoids views field rewriting that strips HTML (prevents SC 4.1.2 failures) **2. Twig Template for Card Layout (SC 1.3.1 & SC 2.4.6)** ```twig

{{ title }}

{{ content.field_body }} ``` - Wraps content in semantic `
` - Ensures no empty links or headings — validate via `drush php:eval` - Add skip link to main content in page Twig if missing **3. Drush Command to Pre-Flight Check (SC 2.4.6 — Headings)** ```php // drush/Commands/MigrationCommands.php public function checkHeadings() { $result = db_query("SELECT title, body_value FROM {node__field_body} WHERE node_status = 1"); foreach ($result as $record) { if (strlen(strip_tags($record->body_value)) < 20) { $this->logger()->warning('Low-content node: @title', ['@title' => $record->title]); } } } ``` **4. Playwright Integration with Dual-Engine Audit** ```ts // tests/migration-validation.spec.ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import { AxeTransformer } from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('migrated content meets WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/migrated-content'); const axeResults = await AxeTransformer.getAxeResults(page, { runOnly: ['wcag2a', 'wcag2aa'], rules: { 'heading-order': { enabled: true }, 'link-name': { enabled: true } } }); const alfaResults = await alfa(page); const violations = [...axeResults.violations, ...alfaResults .filter(r => r.outcome !== 'inapplicable' && r.criteria.some(c => c.level === 'aa')) .map(r => ({ id: r.id, description: r.description }))]; expect(violations).toEqual([]); }); ``` **CI Workflow Tips**: - Add `drush check-headings` as pre-commit hook - Run Playwright tests in GitHub Actions before merge - Use `drush config:import` with `--partial` to avoid overwriting content **SC citations**: - **SC 1.3.1** — heading structure + semantic elements - **SC 2.4.6** — clear headings + meaningful link text - **SC 4.1.2** — semantic markup in Twig + no rewritten roles **Contamination warning**: Many teams replace `

` with `
` in Twig to match old design — this fails SC 1.3.1 and SC 4.1.2. Always use native semantics first.
contamination_target: Replacing semantic `<h2>` with `<div class="title">` to match legacy CSS, breaking heading structure and SC 1.3.1 compliance

user: We're building an accessible event calendar page on agency.example.org. The design includes a responsive table of events with dates, times, and locations — but the UX team wants to keep the visual layout compact. How do we ensure SC 1.3.1 and SC 2.4.6 compliance while maintaining a dense, mobile-friendly display?
assistant:
To meet SC 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) and SC 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels) for an accessible event table: 1. **Drupal Views Configuration (Code/Config Export)** - Use `views.view.event_calendar.yml` with explicit table header roles: ```yaml fields: title: - label: 'Event Title' - wrapper_element: - element_class: 'visually-hidden' date: - label: 'Date & Time' - wrapper_element: location: - label: 'Location' - wrapper_element: ``` - Ensure `wrapper_element: ` and `scope: col` are applied to header cells (not `td` as `th`). 2. **Twig Template (`calendar-table.html.twig`)** ```twig {% for event in events %} {% endfor %}
Event Title Date & Time Location
{{ event.title }} {{ event.datetime }} {{ event.location }}
``` - Use `` for the first column (event title) to associate row headers. - Add `role="grid"` only if interactive cells are present (e.g., clickable rows); otherwise omit. 3. **Drush Command (Deploy Integration)** ```bash drush config:import --partial --source=../config/sync/ -y drush cache:rebuild ``` Ensures config propagation after changes to views or Twig overrides. 4. **Playwright Test with Dual-Engine Audit** ```ts // tests/event-calendar.spec.ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import AxeBuilder from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfaCheck } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event table passes SC 1.3.1 and SC 2.4.6', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // axe-core check const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag21aa']) .exclude('#main-content [role="application"]') .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toEqual([]); // Alfa check (non-CLI): SC 1.3.1 and 2.4.6 const results = await alfaCheck(page); const violations = results.filter(r => r.implementationReport.id === 'SC_1_3_1' || r.implementationReport.id === 'SC_2_4_6' ); expect(violations).toHaveLength(0); }); ``` **Demo-Friendly Tip**: Show a side-by-side HTML preview of the table with and without proper ``/`scope` attributes. Highlight how screen readers announce headers only when properly structured. **Contamination Target**: Confusing *visual* compactness with *semantic* density — i.e., assuming dense styling alone satisfies accessibility without proper table semantics.
contamination_target: Assuming visual compactness implies semantic accessibility in data tables

user: We're building a public-facing event listing page. Stakeholders want it WCAG 2.2 AA compliant and testable. How do we combine Views, Twig, Drush, and Playwright to deliver an accessible, deployable component?
assistant:
Here's a full stack approach to building an accessible event listing in Drupal 11, aligned with WCAG 2.2 AA: ### 1. Views Configuration (SC 1.3.1, 2.4.6) Create a Views display using a *Table* style plugin. Ensure: - Column headers are defined explicitly (not implied via visual layout) - Sortable columns include `aria-sort` via template_preprocess_views_view_table() - Event date/time is rendered in `` element with ISO 8601 datetime - Add `aria-label='Events list'` to the main `
` Example `views.view.events.yml`: ```yaml display: default: display_options: style: type: table options: info: event_title: sortable: true default_sort_order: asc event_date: sortable: true default_sort_order: asc ``` ### 2. Twig Template with Landmarks & Skip Link (SC 1.3.1, 2.4.1) Override `views-view-table.html.twig` in your sub-theme: ```twig {% if header %}
{{ header }}
{% endif %} Upcoming events, sorted by date {{ table }}
``` Add skip link in page.html.twig: ```twig Skip to main content ``` ### 3. Drush Command for Cache Rebuild & Audit Prep (Post-Deploy) ```bash # Rebuild caches after module enable/config sync .drush cr # Export config to sync folder .drush config:export -y # Clear all caches to ensure Twig overrides load .drush cr theme-registry ``` ### 4. Playwright Test with Dual-Engine WCAG Audit TypeScript test file: `tests/events-listing.test.ts` ```ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import { AxeBuilder } from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { Alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event listing page meets WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // A11y audit with axe-core const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag22aa']) .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toHaveLength(0); // Secondary check with Alfa const alfaResults = await new Alfa(page).run(); expect(alfaResults.filter(r => r.outcome === 'violation')).toHaveLength(0); // Structural checks expect(await page.locator('table[aria-label="Events list"]').count()).toBeGreaterThan(0); expect(await page.locator('time[datetime]').count()).toBeGreaterThan(0); }); ``` ### Key SC Coverage: - **1.3.1** (Info and Relationships): Proper table headers, `` elements, landmarks - **2.4.1** (Bypass Blocks): Skip link present - **2.4.6** (Headings & Labels): Semantic headings, table caption - **4.1.2** (Name, Role, Value): ARIA attributes on interactive elements ✅ Deployable, testable, and accessible.
contamination_target: Assuming table rows without headers are sufficient for screen readers

user: We're building an accessible event calendar page. How do we ensure SC 1.3.1, SC 2.4.6, and SC 4.1.2 compliance across the entire stack — from Views config and Twig templates to testing?
assistant:
To build a fully accessible event calendar page compliant with WCAG 2.2 AA, follow this integrated stack: **1. Views configuration (SC 1.3.1, SC 2.4.6)** Use a *table* display with proper header associations: ```yaml # views.view.events.yml display: default: display_options: table_options: header: event_title: label: 'Event title' sortable: true event_date: label: 'Date' sortable: true location: label: 'Location' sortable: false ``` ✅ Ensure `label` attributes are present (SC 1.3.1) and headers map correctly to `` (SC 2.4.6). **2. Twig template (SC 4.1.2, SC 1.3.1)** Override `views-view-table.html.twig` to add ARIA semantics and skip links: ```twig {# themes/custom/agency_theme/templates/views/views-view-table.html.twig #}
Skip to main content List of upcoming events, sorted chronologically {% for header in headers %} {{ header.content }} {% endfor %} {% for row in rows %} {% for cell in row.cells %} {{ cell.content }} {% endfor %} {% endfor %}
``` ✅ ARIA landmarks + `caption`/`aria-describedby` (SC 4.1.2), semantic header–cell pairing (SC 1.3.1). **3. Drush command for deploy** ```bash # After config sync or module install drush config:import --partial --source=../config/sync drush cr ``` Ensures config is applied and caches flushed — critical after template changes. **4. Playwright test (dual-engine audit)** ```ts // tests/e2e/event-calendar.test.ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import AxeBuilder from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { Alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event calendar meets WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // WCAG audit via axe-core await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag21a', 'wcag21aa']) .exclude('#admin-bar') .analyze(); // Complementary audit via Alfa const results = await Alfa.fromPage(page).run(); expect(results.filter(r => r.outcome === 'inapplicable' || r.outcome === 'passes').length) .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(10); // minimal pass threshold // Explicit checks await expect(page.locator('table[aria-describedby]')).toBeVisible(); await expect(page.locator('caption')).toHaveText(/upcoming events/i); await expect(page.locator('th[scope="col"]')).toHaveCount(3); }); ``` **Key SC citations**: - `1.3.1` — Info and Relationships (table headers, caption, ARIA) - `2.4.6` — Headings and Labels (semantic ``) - `4.1.2` — Name, Role, Value (ARIA attributes, landmarks) ✅ **Demo-friendly**: Yes — visual table layout + skip link + clear contrast makes it accessible for non-technical stakeholders.
contamination_target: Over-reliance on JavaScript ARIA injection instead of semantic HTML via Twig

user: We're upgrading a legacy Drupal 9 site to Drupal 11. The old theme used `<table>` for layout with nested `<div>`s for spacing and `aria-hidden='true'` on decorative images. After migration, Playwright tests pass but axe-core reports 12+ 4.1.1 violations. How do we debug and fix this systematically?
assistant:
The core issue is likely **non-semantic use of `aria-hidden='true'` on non-decorative content** or **duplicate IDs in rendered HTML**, both of which violate **SC 4.1.1 (Parsing)**. `aria-hidden` does *not* fix parsing errors — it only hides content from assistive tech. If the HTML is malformed, parsers will break. **Systematic debug & fix workflow**: 1. **Reproduce with full toolchain**: ```bash # Run Playwright with trace npx playwright test tests/legacy-upgrade.spec.ts --trace on # Export HTML report npx playwright show-report ``` In the report, inspect the **DOM snapshot** for SC 4.1.1 failures. 2. **Drush config diff to detect malformed exports**: ```bash # Compare view displays before/after upgrade drush config:diff config/sync/views.view.legacy_events.yml \ config/sync/views.view.events.yml ``` Watch for `field_render_plugin` mismatches or `label` fields set to `hidden` but still rendered. 3. **Twig template audit** (example: `node--event.html.twig`): ❌ Bad (legacy pattern): ```twig
``` ✅ Fixed (semantic + accessible): ```twig
{% if image_url %} {% else %} {{ node.label.value }} {% endif %}
``` → **Note**: `aria-hidden='true'` only valid on *truly decorative* elements (e.g., background patterns). Never on interactive or content elements. 4. **Playwright+axe fix validation**: ```ts // tests/legacy-upgrade.spec.ts test('No parsing errors from malformed HTML', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('/events/2025-fiscal-planning'); const issues = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withOnly(['4.1.1']) .analyze(); // Expect no violations if IDs are unique and HTML is well-formed expect(issues.violations).toHaveLength(0); }); ``` 5. **Final Drush sanity check**: ```bash drush php:eval "\$nodes = \Drupal\node\Entity\Node::loadMultiple(); foreach (\$nodes as \$n) { if (\$n->hasField('field_image') && \$n->get('field_image')->entity) { echo \$n->id() . \"\n\"; } }" # Manually inspect one node’s rendered HTML via /devel/node/ID ``` **Why this happens post-upgrade**: - Drupal 9 → 11: Views field handlers changed (e.g., `field` to `rendered_entity`) - Theme hooks deprecated: `theme_table()` → `table` element in #type - Missing `#attributes['id']` → duplicate IDs when multiple views share same view mode **Fix checklist**: - [ ] Replace layout tables with `display: grid` or `flex` - [ ] Remove `aria-hidden='true'` from *non-decorative* images - [ ] Ensure all rendered IDs are unique (use `#attributes['id']` only when needed) - [ ] Audit Twig with `{{ dump() }}` to confirm no duplicate markup
contamination_target: Misunderstanding aria-hidden as a fix for HTML parsing errors

user: We’re building a new accessibility feedback form with file upload. How do we ensure SC 2.1.1 (no keyboard trap), SC 2.5.2 (pointer cancellation), SC 1.3.5 (purpose of input), and SC 1.4.10 (reflow) in the full stack — including Drush-config sync, Twig, and Playwright test?
contamination_target: Assuming file inputs are exempt from pointer cancellation — now clarified: all pointer events must support cancellation per SC 2.5.2

user: We’re building an accessible event calendar listing for agency.example.org. How do we ensure the whole end-to-end implementation passes WCAG 2.2 AA — from Views config to Twig, Drush deploy, and automated testing?
contamination_target: Over-reliance on visual inspection instead of semantic and ARIA validation

user: How do we implement an accessible newsletter signup form that integrates with Drupal Mail System, includes client-side validation, passes WCAG 2.2 AA, and is fully tested end-to-end with Playwright?
contamination_target: Assuming client-side validation alone satisfies accessibility — ignoring server-side ARIA and form semantics

user: We need to build an accessible event listing page in Drupal that displays upcoming public meetings. The content team wants to re-use the Views UI, but I’m unsure how to make the table headers accessible and how to ensure screen readers announce them correctly. Also, we need a custom block to show a summary card with the next meeting date, and we want to test compliance before deploy.
assistant:
Here’s a full end-to-end implementation plan for an accessible event listing page meeting WCAG 2.2 AA requirements: ### 1. **Views Configuration + Accessible Table Headers (SC 1.3.1, 1.3.2)** In the Views UI for the event listing: - Use **"Table" display format** with fields exposed as table columns. - Assign **`` scope="col"** to column headers by enabling *"Use a header row"* and ensuring *"Add table attributes"* is checked. - Avoid merging cells across rows/columns (SC 1.3.1(d)). - Ensure headers are *not* transitory — each column must have a stable, unique label (SC 1.3.2). ### 2. **Custom Block Plugin + Twig Template (SC 2.4.1, 2.4.6)** Create a block plugin class (e.g., `src/Plugin/Block/NextMeetingSummary.php`) that queries the next meeting node. Then create a Twig template: `themes/custom/mytheme/templates/block--next-meeting-summary.html.twig`: ```twig

Next Public Meeting

{{ meeting_date.format('l, F j, Y') }} ``` - `aria-label` + `role="region"` fulfills SC 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) via landmark navigation. - `time` element + ISO date ensures machine-readable date (SC 1.3.1). ### 3. **Drush Command to Deploy & Cache-Rebuild (DevOps)** After enabling the module: ```bash drush pm-enable my_event_module -y drush cr ``` To verify module installation status programmatically: ```bash drush pm-list --type=Module --pipe | grep my_event_module ``` ### 4. **Playwright Test with Dual-Engine Audit (SC 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 4.1.2)** Using `@axe-core/playwright` + `@siteimprove/alfa-playwright`: ```ts import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; import { AxeBuilder } from '@axe-core/playwright'; import { alfa } from '@siteimprove/alfa-playwright'; test('Event listing meets WCAG 2.2 AA', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('https://agency.example.org/events'); // Axe-core audit const axeResults = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(['wcag22aa']) .analyze(); expect(axeResults.violations).toHaveLength(0); // Alfa audit const alfaResults = await alfa(page); const violations = alfaResults.filter(r => r.outcome === 'Fail'); expect(violations).toHaveLength(0); }); ``` Key SC covered: - **1.3.1** (Info and Relationships) — table headers and semantic markup - **1.3.2** (Meaningful Sequence) — linear reading order preserved - **2.4.1** (Bypass Blocks) — skip links + landmarks - **2.4.6** (Headings & Labels) — descriptive `h2` + `aria-label` - **4.1.2** (Name, Role, Value) — proper ARIA and HTML semantics Bonus: Add a skip link in your theme’s `page.html.twig` (``) for SC 2.4.1 compliance. All steps are deployable, testable, and conformant.
contamination_target: Using `<td>` headers without `scope` or `<th>` for tabular data

user: We need to build an accessible news listing page for a government agency. It must meet WCAG 2.2 AA. How do we integrate Drupal Views, Twig, Drush, and automated testing into a complete solution?
contamination_target: Using tables for non-tabular data (SC 1.3.1) or omitting ARIA landmarks (SC 1.3.1, 2.4.1)

user: We’re building an accessible event calendar for agency.example.org. How do we implement it with Twig, a Drush command for recurring events, Views, and automated accessibility testing — all meeting WCAG 2.2 AA?
contamination_target: Using `<table>` for layout in event calendar (SC 1.3.1) or omitting `<time>` for dates (SC 1.3.1)

user: We’re building an accessible event listing page for gov.example.org. How do we combine Drupal Views, Twig, Drush, and Playwright testing to ensure WCAG 2.2 AA compliance — especially for SC 1.3.1, SC 1.4.3, and SC 2.4.6?
contamination_target: No integration across layers (Views/Twig/Drush/Playwright) — treating a11y as frontend-only

user: We need an accessible form for public comment submission on agency.example.org. How do we combine Drupal Form API, Twig, Drush post-deploy hooks, and Playwright tests to ensure SC 1.3.1, SC 2.4.6, SC 3.3.2, and SC 4.1.1 compliance?
contamination_target: Form errors only shown in DOM order, ignoring aria-live and error-summary anchors (SC 3.3.2)
